title: Hieracium campylodon
intro: Hieracium campylodon, also known as Rough Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium campylodon include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Rough Hawkweed is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and ointments for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Hieracium campylodon is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The classification of Hieracium campylodon is further divided into several sub-species, but it is generally classified as a perennial herb. The plant is known to grow in a variety of habitats, including meadows, rocky outcrops, and along roadsides. Its classification as a member of the Hieracium genus, which includes hawkweeds, is based on its unique characteristics and growth habits.

benefits: Hieracium campylodon, a lesser-known member of the dandelion family, is said to have potential health benefits due to its rich content of vitamins and minerals.
